claims.claim_text: Marsh & McLennan's global insurance brokerage scale - with $6.6B in Risk & Insurance Services revenue (2013) and $5.7B in Consulting revenue - enables it to negotiate superior terms with a larger pool of global insurers, access proprietary data analytics platforms, and serve multinational clients across geographies that smaller brokers cannot. Underlying revenue growth exceeded expense growth in 22 of 23 consecutive quarters, demonstrating sustained operating leverage.
evidence_quotes: Underlying revenue growth was 4%, exceeding the increase in underlying operating expenses as it has for 22 of the past 23 quarters, a record that stands out in the industry." (Q4 2013, Daniel Glaser)

In Risk and Insurance Services, revenue for 2013 was $6.6 billion, with underlying revenue growth of 3% at Marsh and 5% at Guy Carpenter. Adjusted operating income increased 11% to $1.5 billion." (Q4 2013, Daniel Glaser)
Since 2007, adjusted operating income has more than tripled and the adjusted margin has expanded by more than 1,300 basis points." (Q4 2013, Daniel Glaser)
claims.notes: Scale is real but Marsh competes directly with AON (similar scale) and Willis Towers Watson. The 22/23 quarters of revenue exceeding expense growth is a strong structural discipline indicator. Score 0.5 - scale advantage shared with peer group.

claims.claim_text: Marsh's insurance brokerage relationships are deeply embedded in clients' risk management programs. Marsh generated $1.1 billion in new business annually (a record) while maintaining high retention rates, demonstrating that client switching is costly. Guy Carpenter achieved organic revenue growth for 20 consecutive quarters (Q4 2013) through client relationships that extend beyond transaction brokerage to advisory/analytics - creating high switching costs in complex reinsurance programs.
evidence_quotes: Marsh continued to produce strong levels of new business, with $1.1 billion for the year, a new annual record." (Q4 2013, Daniel Glaser)

Guy Carpenter has grown organically for 20 straight quarters with average annual underlying revenue growth of 5%. As a result of building headwinds... we currently expect modest underlying revenue growth at Guy Carpenter in 2014." (Q4 2013, Daniel Glaser)

The C-Suites of reinsurance companies come to rely very heavily on the advising capabilities, analytic capabilities, relationship of Guy Carpenter. So it's a more balanced organization than it was in the past." (Q4 2013, Daniel Glaser)
claims.notes: 20 consecutive quarters of organic growth at Guy Carpenter despite industry headwinds demonstrates embedded client relationships. Advisory role creates higher switching costs than pure transaction brokerage. Score 0.5.

claims.claim_text: Mercer's investment management business reached $136B in AUM (Q2 2015), growing 8% in investments revenue in Q2 2015, with Mercer Marketplace (private health exchange) launched as a separate division to capitalize on ACA-driven demand for benefits outsourcing. The combination of $12.3B revenue, global footprint, and human capital analytics creates an advisory advantage at scale for multinational corporations.
evidence_quotes: Mercer's revenue was $1 billion, an underlying increase of 4%. Revenue grew across major geographies... All global businesses contributed, led by investments at 8%, with AUM now reaching $136 billion." (Q2 2015, Daniel Glaser)

We have a permanent dissatisfaction with the status quo, and we act with integrity and purpose in all that we do. Our continued commitment to these principles should rank us among elite global growth companies." (Q2 2015, Daniel Glaser)
For the year, we delivered underlying revenue growth of 3%, while managing underlying adjusted expense growth to 1%." (Q4 2013, Daniel Glaser)
claims.notes: Mercer Marketplace as a private health exchange platform creates network effects (more employers → more insurer negotiating power) but is still an early-stage business. $136B AUM is a scale advantage in investment consulting. Score 0.5.

claims.claim_text: Oliver Wyman (MMC consulting subsidiary) accumulated proprietary expertise in financial services, aviation, transportation, and energy sectors built over decades of engagements. Oliver Wyman delivered mid-teens growth in 2014 and continued growth in H1 2015 by leveraging this cross-industry expertise and brand in strategy consulting. The depth of sector-specific expertise creates a Process Power barrier that new entrants cannot quickly replicate.
evidence_quotes: Oliver Wyman's underlying revenue growth of 5% for the 6 months as it builds on mid-teens growth last year. Revenue increases were achieved across all businesses." (Q2 2015, Daniel Glaser)
Oliver Wyman's 5% increase in underlying revenue was its strongest performance in the past 6 quarters, reflecting significant growth in financial services." (Q4 2013, Daniel Glaser)
claims.notes: Strategy consulting expertise is difficult to verify from transcripts alone - the claim is primarily assertion-based. Oliver Wyman competes with McKinsey, BCG, Bain which also have deep sector expertise. Score 0.3 for industry-competitive consultancy environment.

claims.claim_text: ACA healthcare reform, employer plan complexity, and global risk complexity trends create structural CAGR tailwinds for MMC's insurance brokerage (Marsh), reinsurance (Guy Carpenter), and benefits consulting (Mercer) businesses. Management targets 13% CAGR in EPS over the long term, with year-over-year adjusted EPS growth of 13%, 16%, and 15% in 2011, 2012, and 2013, respectively.
evidence_quotes: We are well positioned to deliver on our long-term goals, which should lead to superior returns for our shareholders." (Q4 2013, Daniel Glaser)
Adjusted EPS rose 13% in 2011, 16% in 2012 and 15% this past year. And over the 3-year period, the adjusted operating margin improved 330 basis points." (Q4 2013, Daniel Glaser)
We remain confident in our ability to grow EPS at a 13% CAGR over the long term." (Q2 2015, Daniel Glaser)
claims.notes: Consistent 13-16% EPS CAGR over 3 years and 13% long-term target is specific and credible. ACA complexity and global risk management trends are genuine tailwinds. Score 0.5 - macro-driven partly but also company-specific execution.

key_themes: Revenue exceeding expense growth in 22/23 consecutive quarters (Scale Economies discipline)
Guy Carpenter 20 consecutive quarters of organic growth (Switching Costs)
Mercer $136B investment AUM and Mercer Marketplace platform
Oliver Wyman mid-teens growth in 2014 (Process Power)
13% long-term EPS CAGR target with 3-year track record
extraction_summary.assessment: MRSH shows consistent financial execution with operating leverage discipline (22/23 quarters), but structural competitive advantages are moderate in an industry where AON and Willis Towers Watson have similar scale and capabilities.
